title: Macrame wall hanging: sunrise
subtitle: A semicircular wall hanging with radiating cords in two colours, using square knots and lark's head mounts
excerpt: This wall hanging uses a wooden dowel or driftwood as a mount, with cords radiating outward from a central point to create a fan or sunrise shape. The two-colour design uses alternating cords in natural cotton and a dyed cord so the knot pattern creates a sunray effect. The finished hanging is approximately 50 cm wide and 40 cm deep.
sourceNotes: The sunrise wall hanging format is a widely-produced contemporary macramé pattern type. Method follows standard lark's head mount and square knot practice as taught in UK macramé workshops.

content.text: The sunrise shape comes from mounting cords across the full dowel width and then angling successive rows of knots downward toward a central point. The
gives each cord pair a clean, even start on the dowel. The pattern below uses alternating natural and coloured cords: the colour distribution through the
rows creates the sunray gradient without requiring any cutting or reattaching mid-project.

items.name: 3 mm single-strand cotton macramé cord, natural
3 mm single-strand cotton macramé cord, dyed (mustard or rust)
Comb or wide-tooth pet brush for fringing

content.text: Step 1: Cut and mount the cords

Cut 16 natural cords at 200 cm and 8 dyed cords at 200 cm. Fold each in half and mount with lark's head knots along the dowel: alternate two natural, one dyed, two natural, one dyed across the full width. You will have 48 working cord ends (32 natural, 16 dyed).
Step 2: Work the square knot rows

Work square knots across all cord groups in rows: each group of 4 adjacent cords makes one square knot. Work 3 full rows of square knots straight across, then begin offsetting: drop the outermost 2 cords from each side on each subsequent row to create the fanning, angled shape. The offset rows create the radiating lines of the sunrise silhouette.

Trim the hanging cords to a curved fringe that mirrors the fan shape: shortest in the centre, longest at the outer edges. Unravel the cord ends with a comb or pet brush to create a fluffy, brushed fringe. Trim again after brushing to neaten the silhouette.

items.fix: After each square knot, hold the filler cords taut and allow the working cords to lie across without tension before completing the knot. Even tension produces flat, even rows.
items.cause: Working cords pulled too tight when tying knots
items.problem: The square knot rows pull inward and the hanging is narrower at the bottom than the top

items.fix: Begin the angled offset rows by row 4 at the latest. Each offset row should drop 2-4 cords from each side to create a visible fan shape by the midpoint of the piece.
items.cause: Insufficient cord drop between the straight rows and the offset rows
items.problem: The sunrise fan shape is not visible and the hanging looks like a rectangle
